Politics.headline: Trump Attacks Iran: What will Congress do?
Politics.detail: The joint operation between the United States and Israel against Iran marks a significant shift from Trump's previous reluctance regarding foreign conflicts initiated by unpopular presidents, prompting questions about the congressional response.

Politics.headline: Trump Says Iran War Will Be Over "Very Soon"
Politics.detail: President Trump told House Republicans that the war in Iran will be a short-term event, attempting to alleviate concerns about a prolonged conflict in the Middle East.

Politics.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Iran's late supreme leader, is chosen to replace his father.
Politics.detail: Iran has appointed Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the former Supreme Leader, as his successor.

Politics.headline: Ex-Missouri house speaker sentenced 21 months for misusing Covid relief funds.
Politics.detail: The former speaker of the Missouri House has been sentenced to 21 months in prison for misusing COVID-19 relief funds.

Politics.headline: Alexander Butterfield, Nixon aide who disclosed Watergate tapes, dies aged 99.
Politics.detail: Alexander Butterfield, a former Nixon aide who revealed the existence of the Watergate tapes, has died at the age of 99.

Technology.headline: ChatGPT Now Identifies and Remembers Songs With New Shazam App
Technology.detail: ChatGPT has integrated a new Shazam app that allows it to identify and remember songs.

Technology.headline: Thousands of authors publish 'empty' book in protest over AI using their work
Technology.detail: Thousands of authors are protesting against AI using their work by publishing empty books.

Technology.headline: AI vs AI: Agent hacked McKinsey's chatbot and gained full read-write access in just two hours
Technology.detail: An AI agent successfully hacked McKinsey's chatbot, gaining full read-write access in just two hours, highlighting potential security vulnerabilities.

Technology.headline: New Apple 'HomePad' Leak Tips 2026 Launch, MagSafe-Like Wall Mount
Technology.detail: A leak suggests that Apple is planning to launch a new 'HomePad' in 2026, featuring a MagSafe-like wall mount.

Technology.headline: Google's New AI Features Are Trying to Make Data Entry a Thing of the Past
Technology.detail: Google is introducing new AI features aimed at streamlining and automating data entry processes.

Science.headline: This 2-Pound Dinosaur Is Rewriting What Scientists Know About Evolution
Science.detail: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing other bird-like features.

Science.headline: Scientists May Have Discovered a Brand-New Mineral on Mars
Science.detail: Scientists studying Mars may have uncovered a brand-new mineral hidden in the planet's ancient sulfate deposits. Researchers identified an unusual iron sulfate—ferric hydroxysulfate—forming in layered deposits near the massive Valles Marineris canyon system, likely formed when sulfate-rich deposits left behind by ancient water were later heated by volcanic or geothermal activity.

Science.headline: Cosmic Voids Look Empty but They May Be Tearing the Universe Apart
Science.detail: Cosmic voids may seem like the emptiest places in the universe but they're far from nothing. Even in these vast empty regions, the fundamental quantum fields that fill all of space remain, carrying a small but real amount of energy known as vacuum energy, or dark energy which is dominant in these voids.

Science.headline: Falling meteorite smashes hole in roof of German house after spectacular 'fireball' explosion over Europe
Science.detail: A falling meteorite smashed a hole in the roof of a German house after a spectacular 'fireball' explosion over Europe. This event highlights the ongoing occurrence of meteoroid impacts on Earth and the potential for these space rocks to reach the surface.

Science.headline: Striking new image of 3I/ATLAS from Jupiter-bound spacecraft
Science.detail: The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a new image of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS that the Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer (JUICE) spacecraft took on its way to Jupiter. The image reveals a bright halo of gas – the coma – surrounding the much smaller nucleus.

Health.headline: Colorblindness May Hide Life-Threatening Warning Sign
Health.detail: Researchers analyzing millions of medical records found that people with colorblindness may be masking a potentially dangerous health condition.

Health.headline: Alzheimer's Care Gap Between Urban and Rural Communities
Health.detail: A new analysis reveals a significant disparity in Alzheimer's care between urban and rural areas in Maryland, with rural regions lacking necessary resources and specialists.

Health.headline: Lab-Grown Beef May Pose Risk for Alpha-Gal Syndrome
Health.detail: Proteomic analysis suggests that lab-grown beef has a different allergen profile than conventional meat, with potential implications for individuals with alpha-gal syndrome.

Health.headline: Hospitalists Get Creative to Find Time for Research
Health.detail: Hospitalists are devising innovative strategies to carve out time for research activities amidst their demanding clinical responsibilities.

Health.headline: Risk Factors for Early Development of Food Allergies
Health.detail: Recent research has identified key risk factors that contribute to the early development of food allergies in infants and young children.

Sports.headline: Draper sets up Djokovic clash after beating Cerundolo at Indian Wells
Sports.detail: Jack Draper defeated Francisco Cerundolo to set up a fourth-round clash with Novak Djokovic in the BNP Paribas Open at Indian Wells.

Sports.headline: NBA cancels Atlanta Hawks' theme night with strip club Magic City after backlash
Sports.detail: The NBA cancelled the Atlanta Hawks' theme night that was to involve the strip club Magic City following public criticism.

Sports.headline: Australia grants asylum to 5 members of the Iranian women's soccer team
Sports.detail: Five members of the Iranian women's soccer team have been granted asylum in Australia.

Sports.headline: NFL Highest-Paid Wide Receivers: Alec Pierce Joins Top 10 With Huge Contract
Sports.detail: Alec Pierce has signed a huge contract with the Colts, making him one of the top 10 highest-paid wide receivers in the NFL.

Sports.headline: Free agent safety Alohi Gilman has agreed to a three-year, $24.75 million deal with the Kansas City Chiefs
Sports.detail: Alohi Gilman is heading to the Kansas City Chiefs on a 3-year deal for $24.75 million.

Entertainment.headline: Elizabeth Hurley wows in plunging Versace dress she first wore 27 years ago
Entertainment.detail: Elizabeth Hurley recently turned heads in a Versace dress she originally wore 27 years prior, showcasing her timeless style.

Entertainment.headline: James Van Der Beek's daughter, 9, offers moving advice about grief: 'I just tell him about my day'
Entertainment.detail: James Van Der Beek's 9-year-old daughter shared heartfelt advice about coping with grief, suggesting talking about one's day to remember loved ones.

Entertainment.headline: 'Mormon Wives' star says plastic surgery nightmare ruined her life and reality TV career
Entertainment.detail: A star from 'Mormon Wives' claims that a plastic surgery nightmare negatively impacted her life and career in reality television.

Entertainment.headline: Boston lead singer Tommy DeCarlo dead at 60
Entertainment.detail: Tommy DeCarlo, the lead singer of the band Boston, has passed away at the age of 60.

Entertainment.headline: Rihanna's Beverly Hills home involved in 'targeted shooting' as police make arrest
Entertainment.detail: Rihanna is reportedly unharmed after a woman allegedly fired multiple shots at her Beverly Hills home; an arrest has been made.

Crime & Justice.headline: Owner of Durable Medical Equipment Company Sentenced for $59M Medicare Fraud
Crime & Justice.detail: The owner of a durable medical equipment company was sentenced for a $59 million Medicare fraud scheme. The scheme involved submitting fraudulent claims to Medicare for equipment that was not medically necessary or was never provided.

Crime & Justice.headline: Two ISIS Supporters Charged with Attempting to Detonate Explosive Devices During Protests Outside Gracie Mansion
Crime & Justice.detail: Two individuals who support ISIS have been charged with attempting to detonate explosive devices during protests outside of Gracie Mansion. The suspects face federal charges related to terrorism.

Crime & Justice.headline: Pennsylvania Brothers Convicted of Decades Long Racketeering Conspiracy
Crime & Justice.detail: Two brothers from Pennsylvania have been convicted of engaging in a racketeering conspiracy that spanned decades. The conviction marks the end of a long investigation into their criminal activities.

Crime & Justice.headline: FBI launches terrorism probe into IED attack outside NYC mayor's residence
Crime & Justice.detail: The FBI has launched a terrorism probe into an IED attack outside of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's residence. Two men from Pennsylvania are facing federal charges in connection with the incident.

Crime & Justice.headline: US government contractor accused of stealing $46M arrested on small island, FBI announces
Crime & Justice.detail: A former US government contractor accused of stealing $46 million in cryptocurrency from the U.S. Marshals Service has been arrested on a small island by the FBI and French authorities. The contractor is facing charges related to the theft.

Education.headline: Teaching-only and temporary roles bear brunt of UK jobs cull
Education.detail: Universities in the UK are cutting jobs, with teaching-focused and temporary positions being disproportionately affected. This trend raises concerns about the future of academic careers and the quality of education.

Education.headline: Canada-India reset 'not about restoring student numbers'
Education.detail: Efforts to improve relations between Canada and India are not primarily focused on increasing the number of Indian students studying in Canada. The focus is on broader diplomatic and educational cooperation.

Education.headline: French science 'on worrying trajectory' after budget cuts
Education.detail: Budget cuts in France are negatively impacting the science sector, causing concern about the future of research and development. The cuts may lead to reduced opportunities for students and researchers.

Education.headline: Shorten UK undergraduate degrees to just two years, says v-c
Education.detail: A vice-chancellor in the UK is proposing shortening undergraduate degrees to two years. This proposal aims to reduce student debt and make higher education more accessible.

Education.headline: 10 things we learned about Indian unis' internationalisation plans
Education.detail: A recent article highlights key takeaways regarding the internationalization strategies of Indian universities. These strategies are crucial for enhancing the global competitiveness and reputation of Indian institutions.

Lifestyle.headline: 'Charismatic and extremely confident': how to recognise – and handle – a psychopath
Lifestyle.detail: An article discusses how to recognize and handle individuals with psychopathic traits.

Lifestyle.headline: Thomasina Miers' recipe for stuffed cabbage in white wine and escabeche, with buttered dill and pea rice
Lifestyle.detail: A recipe for stuffed cabbage in white wine and escabeche, complemented by buttered dill and pea rice.

Lifestyle.headline: Job Hugging Is The New Workplace Trend. But What Does It Mean?
Lifestyle.detail: An article discusses the emerging trend of 'job hugging' in the workplace and its implications.

Lifestyle.headline: Ancient herb known as 'nature's Valium' touted for improving sleep and anxiety
Lifestyle.detail: An ancient herb is being promoted as a natural remedy for improving sleep and alleviating anxiety.

Lifestyle.headline: 9 supermarket foods nutritionists swear by to add years to your life
Lifestyle.detail: A nutritional therapist shares nine of the best foods to add to your shopping basket for healthy ageing and to increase longevity.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Irene Zhang. Americans are 'Chinamaxxing.' It's not cause for geopolitical panic.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: In an opinion piece, Irene Zhang discusses the trend of 'Chinamaxxing' among Americans and argues that it shouldn't be a cause for geopolitical panic.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Third Spaces': What reassurance in the UAE looks like.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece reflecting on the sense of reassurance found in 'third spaces' within the United Arab Emirates.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: KU Med lawsuits allege deaths linked to contaminated open-heart surgery devices
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Lawsuits are alleging that deaths are linked to contaminated open-heart surgery devices.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Culver City, don't roll back your ambitious safe street redesign.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial board argues against Culver City rolling back its ambitious safe street redesign.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Bullying Anthropic: On AI firm Anthropic versus U.S. government.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses the situation between AI firm Anthropic and the U.S. government.

World.headline: Iran rules out ceasefire talks; US threatens 'most intense day of strikes'
World.detail: Amid the ongoing conflict, Iran has rejected ceasefire negotiations, while the United States is threatening to launch its most intense series of strikes yet. The situation remains tense as both sides continue to escalate their actions.

World.headline: US grants waiver to allow India to buy Russian oil amid Iran war
World.detail: The United States has granted India a waiver to continue purchasing Russian oil despite the ongoing war with Iran. This decision comes as the conflict impacts global oil supplies and prices.

World.headline: EU mobilises emergency humanitarian aid for Lebanon
World.detail: The European Union has activated emergency humanitarian assistance for Lebanon, which includes food, medical supplies, shelter, recreational kits, and winter clothing, in response to the increasing humanitarian needs resulting from recent hostilities and the displacement of over 680,000 people. The aid will be delivered through partners like WFP and UNICEF.
World.source: European Civil Protection and Humanitarian Aid Operations (ECHO)

World.headline: Alexander brothers are convicted of sex trafficking in case that shocked the real estate world
World.detail: Tal and Oren Alexander, prominent real estate brokers, along with their brother Alon, were convicted in Manhattan on charges of sex trafficking. They face potential life sentences following the verdict.

World.headline: China urges parties to stop military operations in the Middle East
World.detail: Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun expressed deep concern over the ongoing tensions in the Middle East and urged all parties to cease military operations to prevent further escalation and a greater impact on global economic growth. China is actively engaged in shuttle diplomacy to mediate and de-escalate the situation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Government vows tougher stance on road quality as Mhona opens rebuilt Harare artery
Zimbabwe.detail: The government is taking a firmer approach to road construction and maintenance, according to recent reports, as Minister Mhona inaugurated a rebuilt road in Harare.

Zimbabwe.headline: Alarm as snakebites hit 1 500 cases in 2026
Zimbabwe.detail: Health officials are raising concerns as snakebite incidents have reached 1,500 cases in 2026, highlighting a growing public health issue.

Zimbabwe.headline: Court case exposes US$3 500 loan 'fee'
Zimbabwe.detail: A recent court case has brought to light a practice of charging a US$3,500 'fee' associated with loan applications, raising questions about financial transparency.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe deepens diplomatic dialogue . . . Engagement fosters transparency, strengthens relationships
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is actively pursuing diplomatic engagement to foster transparency and strengthen relationships with other nations.

Zimbabwe.headline: Kavango Resources raises US$8.4m in UK and Zimbabwe to boost gold operations
Zimbabwe.detail: Kavango Resources has successfully raised US$8.4 million from investors in the UK and Zimbabwe, with the funds intended to bolster its gold mining operations.
